qa_pairs.question: How do I start a JupyterHub desktop session for using COMSOL?
qa_pairs.answer: You can start a JupyterHub desktop session by clicking one of the provided links: for FIR, use `https://jupyterhub.fir.alliancecan.ca`; for NARVAL, use `https://portail.narval.calculquebec.ca/`; and for RORQUAL, use `https://jupyterhub.rorqual.alliancecan.ca`.

qa_pairs.question: What are the steps to launch COMSOL after starting a JupyterHub desktop session?
qa_pairs.answer: After starting a JupyterHub desktop session, you need to highlight a COMSOL module (such as `comsol/6.4`) in the 'Available Module' section on the left-hand side. Then, click 'Load' for the highlighted module, which will make a 'Comsol (VNC)' Icon appear in the desktop. Clicking this icon should automatically start COMSOL in a remote Jupyter desktop.

qa_pairs.question: Which JupyterHub links are available for accessing COMSOL?
qa_pairs.answer: The available JupyterHub links are: FIR (`https://jupyterhub.fir.alliancecan.ca`), NARVAL (`https://portail.narval.calculquebec.ca/`), and RORQUAL (`https://jupyterhub.rorqual.alliancecan.ca`).

qa_pairs.question: What is the Batch Sweep approach in COMSOL GUI?
qa_pairs.answer: The Batch Sweep approach is used for solving parametric problems when working interactively in the COMSOL graphical user interface (GUI). It allows for carrying out multiple parameter sweeps and can also provide speedup due to Task Parallelism.

qa_pairs.question: How are parameter sweeps executed on a cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: To run a parameter sweep on a cluster, a job must be submitted to the scheduler from the command line using the `sbatch slurmscript` command.

qa_pairs.question: Is it possible to submit parametric simulations to a cluster queue from the COMSOL graphical interface?
qa_pairs.answer: No, support for submitting parametric simulations to the cluster queue from the graphical interface using a 'Cluster Sweep node' is not available at this time.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find more information about additional arguments for running parameter sweeps on a cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: For a discussion regarding additional required arguments, you can refer to the COMSOL support knowledge base articles [https://www.comsol.com/support/knowledgebase/1250 a] and [https://www.comsol.com/blogs/how-to-use-job-sequences-to-save-data-after-solving-your-model/ b].
